rationale.R1.6: "Clean this up" canonically targets prose quality: sentence construction, concision, register consistency, and paragraph cohesion. This is the load-bearing responsibility — failure to improve the writing invalidates the delegation. The artifact has several candidates: the abrupt pivot in paragraph 4 (calibration transfer → BLEU → length ratio), the loose connective tissue between paragraphs 3 and 4, and some nominalization-heavy phrasing throughout.
rationale.R1.2: Peripheral only: rewriting sentences must not alter logical dependencies (e.g., the ECE comparison in §2, the single-pass inference claim in §3). Passive constraint, not an active task.
rationale.R1.5: Peripheral only: paragraph 4 bundles three loosely related findings (cross-lingual transfer, BLEU score, length ratio), which a thorough clean-up might lightly restructure, but wholesale reorganization is not implied by the delegation.
